meta.intended_use: Hand-off to another agent to continue design/implementation without losing decisions or rationale.
scope: Homelab storage layout (mini production cluster style)
Mountpoint strategy and intended IO patterns
Back-burner items to finalize (FS choices, backups, WAL simulation, snapshots)
change_log: Corrected boot RAID1 pair to mixed-model Intel SSD Pro 1500 180GB + Intel SSD 520 180GB (both ~90% health).
Added exact mdadm RAID1 partition + mount plan for Ubuntu Server.
Added storage_architecture tier map with explicit mountpoints and directories.
2026-03-15 TASK-0001: Boot tier implemented on alethos-node-01. RAID1 over sda+sdc (md0→/boot, md1→/, md2→/var), dual ESP (sda1, sdc1). NVMe and sdb (HDD) reserved for TASK-0002.

primary_use: Operate observability stack with SLO mindset
Practice production-like reliability patterns on single-node k3s

design_principles.mini_production_cluster_definition: Single host designed like a production cluster via storage/IO/failure-domain separation: OS/control-plane isolation, workload IO isolation, stateful DB isolation, and offline backup/archives isolation.
core_principles: Failure domain separation by storage tier
IO pattern isolation (logs/metrics vs DB vs OS)
Blast radius minimization (mount boundaries + quotas later)
GitOps discipline for configuration where feasible

drives.notes: Label indicates OPAL capability; not used initially.
Consumer-class but healthy; used in RAID1 mirror with Pro 1500.

boot_system_drive.configuration_intent: mdadm RAID1 mirror for OS/system layer
boot_system_drive.reasoning: RAID1 reduces downtime risk from a boot disk failure and isolates OS from workload churn. Mixed-model mirror is acceptable; mirror size equals smallest drive.
boot_system_drive.failure_behavior_goal: Single SSD failure does not take system down; bootable from either disk with GRUB installed on both and duplicated ESP.

platform_tier.device: WD PC SN530 240GB NVMe via PCIe x4 adapter (with heatsink)
role: k3s runtime data (container runtime + kubelet data)
platform_tier.reasoning: Write-heavy observability and container runtime data benefit from NVMe IOPS and must not compete with DB IO.
platform_tier.io_characteristics: High write amplification; high churn; bursty IO.
platform_tier.failure_behavior_goal: If platform tier fills/degrades, OS and DB remain intact; workloads may be impacted but node remains recoverable.

database_tier.device: 1TB NVMe via PCIe x4 adapter (with heatsink)
role: WAL intended isolation (logical separation at mount level)
database_tier.reasoning: Stateful services require low latency and stable throughput; isolation from metrics/logs prevents performance collapse under load.
database_tier.io_characteristics: Latency-sensitive; steady writes; WAL burst potential; compaction/vacuum patterns.
database_tier.failure_behavior_goal: DB tier remains stable even if platform tier is saturated; clear recovery path using backups and WAL strategy.

backup_archive_tier.reasoning: Low-cost capacity and isolation from hot tiers; backups must not compete with primary IO.
backup_archive_tier.io_characteristics: Large sequential writes; low IOPS needs.
backup_archive_tier.failure_behavior_goal: If backup disk fails, system remains running; backups must be restored from off-host copy (AWS s3 -- future).

per_disk_partitions_gpt.name: EFI System Partition
per_disk_partitions_gpt.notes: Primary ESP mounted from disk A; disk B ESP kept in sync via rsync.

per_disk_partitions_gpt.name: VAR
per_disk_partitions_gpt.notes: Blast-radius isolation for logs/system churn; k3s workload dirs will be moved off to NVMe.

boot_resilience_steps: Install GRUB to both disks (/dev/sda and /dev/sdc)
Duplicate/sync EFI partition contents to second disk (sdc1) periodically

must_have_mounts: /var (separate from root to prevent log/container growth from bricking the OS)
notes: Root should remain stable and hard to fill
/var is the typical failure mode; isolate it
k3s workload directories should be redirected off boot tier to NVMe
Platform tier is allowed to churn; protect DB and OS.
Consider bind mounts/symlinks from /var/lib/* paths as needed by k3s/components.
Consider quotas/limits specifically for Prometheus/Loki retention.

target_mounts: /data/postgres_wal (logical WAL isolation; may map to same NVMe physically)
notes: Even if WAL is on same physical NVMe, separating mountpoints prepares for future physical split and enforces operational discipline.
Ensure WAL and data have monitoring and capacity alerts.
Plan for future off-host sync (rsync to another machine or cloud bucket like S3).

cons: Snapshots require LVM or filesystem-level tooling outside ext4
ext4.likely_fit: Best for MVP and simplicity-first approach.

cons: Requires careful planning for boot/root-on-ZFS if chosen
zfs.likely_fit: Best if snapshot/rollback discipline is a top priority and complexity is acceptable.

filesystem_strategy.notes: ext4 is default for simplicity; ZFS considered if snapshot/rollback discipline is prioritized and complexity acceptable.

tier_by_tier_intent.platform_tier: Decision pending (ext4 or ZFS dataset with quotas for prom/loki)
tier_by_tier_intent.database_tier: Decision pending (ext4 with separate mounts vs ZFS datasets; WAL separation policy)
tier_by_tier_intent.backup_tier: Decision pending (ext4 likely; optionally ZFS for snapshot retention)

goals: Define RPO/RTO targets appropriate for homelab

candidate_policy.notes: Subject to storage capacity and AlethosAPI DB size growth.

options: Local streaming replica (second Postgres instance) for practice
WAL archiving + base backups to /backups for PITR simulation

wal_isolation: Use dedicated mountpoint for WAL directory (even if same NVMe initially)
Monitor WAL directory size and write rate
replication_simulation: Option A: create a second Postgres instance (different data dir) to practice streaming replication locally
Option B: use periodic base backups + WAL archiving to /backups to simulate point-in-time recovery (PITR)
Decision depends on complexity tolerance and learning priority

intent: Introduce snapshot/rollback drills (filesystem or volume layer -- probably on ext4)
Requires scheduled rollback drills and documented runbooks
Practice 'data corruption' recovery scenarios (read-only restore, point-in-time if implemented)

must_monitor: Disk usage per mountpoint (/, /var, platform mounts, /data/*, /backups)
IO wait and latency (per device if possible)
Redis: memory usage and persistence settings if enabled

back_burner_tasks: Decide ext4 vs ZFS for each tier
Finalize exact partition + mount plan for NVMe tiers
Backup rotation policy implementation + restore verification automation
WAL archiving vs replication simulation decision
Snapshot + rollback testing discipline (scheduled drills + runbooks)
Codify full storage layout as a mini production cluster architecture doc

current_state_summary: Phase 0A complete: TASK-0001 closed. Boot tier operational—UEFI, mdadm RAID1, dual ESP, headless Ubuntu Server, SSH.
Phase 0B active: TASK-0002—mount /platform (240GB NVMe), /data (1TB NVMe), /backups (1TB HDD); bind mounts from /platform to /var/lib/*; all persistent mounts via UUID.
Platform tier (when mounted) hosts k3s runtime + observability persistence; DB tier isolates Postgres/Redis/WAL from platform IO.
Backup tier on separate physical HDD; backups must not compete with live workloads.
most_important_next_decisions: Complete TASK-0002: filesystem choice per tier if not already fixed (ext4 vs ZFS), partition labels, fstab + bind mount order and verification
ext4 vs ZFS (per tier) for remaining volumes if still open
WAL archiving vs replication simulation approach (later)
non_negotiables: Maintain separation of OS, platform churn, DB, and backups to reduce blast radius
Implement monitoring and disk usage alerts early (operator posture)
Treat restore testing as a required operational practice (not optional)
